intro: Alvordia congesta is a plant species, also known as Oregon Desert Star, belonging to the family Asteraceae, native to the high desert regions of the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Alvordia congesta contains bioactive constituents like sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams to topically treat skin irritations and wounds.
